rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE contract content.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should reference visible ports and features, not implementation-specific subcomponents.
Ensure AGREE expressions use visible identifiers and proper types for declarations.
When defining range constraints, use named constants or visible variables for clarity and reuse.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses assume/guarantee declarations, equation definitions, and assignments within annex agree blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for assignments.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing subcomponent ports in component type annexes; use visible features instead.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing-related tokens such as ms/us/Hz/Bytes
